input.initialPrompt:
I'm on a headless Linux CI server and need to build my MAUI app for Android.
I have the .NET SDK installed but nothing else.
Give me a specific plan to set up the environment, including how to find
the correct versions for everything.

data.content:
Here's a concrete plan for building a .NET MAUI Android app on headless Linux:

---

## 1. Determine Required Versions

```bash
# Check your .NET SDK version — this dictates everything else
dotnet --version

# Check which MAUI workload version your project needs
grep '<TargetFramework>' **/*.csproj
# e.g. net8.0-android → .NET 8, net9.0-android → .NET 9
```

## 2. Install the MAUI / Android Workloads

```bash
# Install the MAUI workload (pulls in android automatically)
sudo dotnet workload install maui-android

# Verify
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install the Java JDK (JDK 17 for .NET 8+)

```bash
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y openjdk-17-jdk
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
```

**How to confirm the right JDK version:** Run `dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n` after workload install — it will error or warn if the JDK version is wrong. Microsoft docs specify JDK 17 for .NET 8/9 MAUI.

## 4. Install Android SDK Command-Line Tools

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME=$HOME/android-sdk
mkdir -p "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools"

# Download command-line tools (check https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only for latest)
wget -q https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-linux-11076708_latest.zip -O cmdline-tools.zip
unzip -q cmdline-tools.zip -d "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools"
mv "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/cmdline-tools" "$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest"

export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH"
```

## 5. Find & Install the Correct Android SDK Packages

```bash
# Check what API level your project targets
grep '<TargetFramework>\|<SupportedOSPlatformVersion>\|<AndroidSdkVersion>' **/*.csproj

# Accept licenses first
yes | sdkmanager --licenses

# Install required components (adjust API 34/35 to match your project)
sdkmanager "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

**How to find the exact build-tools version:** Run `sdkmanager --list | grep build-tools` and pick the one matching your target API level.

## 6. Set Environment Variables

Add these to your CI config or `~/.bashrc`:

```bash
export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-17-openjdk-amd64
export ANDROID_HOME=$HOME/android-sdk
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH"
```

## 7. Build

```bash
# Restore, then build the Android target
dotnet restore
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -c Release

# Or publish a signed APK/AAB
dotnet publish -f net9.0-android -c Release
```

## 8. Troubleshooting

| Problem | Fix |
|---|---|
| `AndroidSDKDirectory not set` | Ensure `ANDROID_HOME` is exported |
| `Java not found` | Ensure `JAVA_HOME` points to JDK 17 |
| `Missing platform API XX` | Run `sdkmanager "platforms;android-XX"` |
| Wrong workload versions | Run `dotnet workload repair` |

**Key version-matching rule:** Your **.NET SDK version** determines the MAUI workload version (automatic), which determines the minimum Android API level and JDK version. Always start from `dotnet --version` and your `.csproj` `<TargetFramework>` to derive everything else.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a specific plan for setting up a headless Linux CI environment to build a .NET MAUI app for Android. They have the .NET SDK but nothing else. Let me provide a comprehensive, actionable plan.
